title: Smoky Roasted Chinese Pork Bao
description: Smoky, spicy, and chewy, this dish is perfect for a midnight craving.

ingredients.name: pork shoulder
ingredients.notes: preferably with a layer of fat

instructions: In a small bowl, mix together brown sugar, smoked paprika, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and oyster sauce.
Rub the spice mixture all over the pork shoulder, making sure to coat it evenly.
Heat the sesame oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat and sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the pork from the pot and add the mirepoix, cooking until the vegetables are tender, about 5 minutes.
Return the pork to the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake the pork for 30 minutes, then reduce the oven temperature to 300°F (150°C) and continue cooking for another 2 hours, or until the pork is tender and falls apart easily.
Shred the pork with two forks and return it to the Dutch oven.
Serve the pork on a bao bun with green onions, sliced scallions, and a drizzle of sesame oil.
Garnish with additional green onions and scallions if desired.
